summary: Ireland's posture is set out by the National Cyber Security Centre, which states that the global shift to post-quantum cryptography is already underway and that Ireland must ensure a secure transition for Government, regulated sectors and critical infrastructure. The NCSC has named post-quantum cryptography as a research and innovation priority but has not published algorithm guidance or a migration timeline.
govActors: NCSC-IE | Ireland's National Cyber Security Centre, national authority for cyber security
provenance: Posture and transition need: NCSC-IE research and innovation priorities page (ingested)
PQC named as a challenge: Director's opening statement to the Oireachtas Joint Committee on Foreign Affairs and Defence, 2023 (ingested)
dominantProcess omitted: no standards camp attested in the documents
